copyright_footer: © 2026 SourceGrid Labs Pte. Ltd. All rights reserved.

home.desc_1: DojoMate is your intuitive mobile app for martial arts academies.
home.desc_2: Easily manage student check-ins, instructor scheduling, and more — all in one clean, simple interface.
home.get_started_text: Press the link to see detailed documentation of the app.
home.support_desc: Reach out to the right team for faster assistance.
home.policies_desc: Understand how we handle data and how training progression works safely and fairly.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.effective_date: Effective date: [to be filled at publish time]
privacy.intro: This Privacy Policy explains how Source Combatives and its related products, including DojoMate, AFL, Education, and related web and mobile services, collect, use, retain, and share information.

scope.title: 1. Scope
items: subscriptions, payments, and related product access
websites, mobile apps, and related support or notification services

collect.title: 2. Information We Collect
collect.intro: We collect different types of information depending on how you use the platform.

account.title: Account and identity information
items: profile details you provide, such as your name and time zone
account.extra_intro: Some features may require additional information, such as:
extra_items: government or national ID for tournament registration, when needed to match the registration to the correct person
account.extra_note: If you do not use tournament registration through DojoMate, you generally do not need to provide this.

attendance.title: Attendance and academy operations data
attendance.intro: We collect data needed to operate academy services, which may include:

payment.title: Payment and subscription data
payment.intro: If you subscribe to paid products or services, we may collect or receive:
payment.note: Payment processing may be handled by third-party providers. We do not describe third-party processor practices in full here, and you should also review the applicable processor terms and privacy materials.

edu.title: Education learning data
edu.intro: If you use Education features, we may collect:

progression.title: Progression, XP, and gamify data
progression.intro: We may collect or derive progression, XP, achievement, streak, and related activity data needed to operate these features for eligible self-managed accounts, including:
items: homework correctness and related progression events
XP, achievements, badges, streaks, accomplishments, and related feature activity
leaderboard participation and public-profile visibility settings where those controls exist
related audit or integrity records used to protect fairness
progression.note: For more detail about progression rules and feature behavior, see

device.title: Device, notification, and security data
items: security and service logs needed to operate and protect the platform

use.title: 3. How We Use Information
use.intro: We use information for the following purposes:

operate.title: To provide and operate the platform
items: powering progression, achievements, and related feature behavior

analytics.title: To maintain analytics and business reporting
items: calculate class-level financial and operational reports
analytics.note: Where possible, long-term analytics may rely on internal identifiers or pseudonymous records instead of human-readable names.

communicate.title: To communicate with users
items: send marketing communications where permitted and configured by the user

personalize.title: To personalize product offers and recommendations
personalize.intro: We may use account activity, such as attendance, subscriptions, Education activity, or progression-related behavior, to personalize recommendations or offers for courses, subscriptions, and related products. Marketing personalization is separate from progression controls, leaderboard participation, and public profile access.
personalize.note: This behavior is separate from core platform operation. Child or dependent data is not used for personalized marketing.

recommendations.title: 4. Recommendations and Offers
recommendations.intro: We may use account activity data to support recommendations and offers related to our products and services.
recommendations.body: This may include activity such as:
recommendations.child_note: Child or dependent data is not used for personalized marketing.
recommendations.aggregate_note: We may also use anonymous or aggregated analytics to understand overall product use and improve the platform.

public_features.title: 5. Public and Community Features
public_features.intro: Some progression, achievement, or community features may involve public or limited public display. In general:
items: leaderboard participation and public profile access are separate controls
opting out of leaderboard participation excludes the user from rankings rather than merely hiding the ranking display
marketing personalization is separate from leaderboard participation and public profile access
competition, bracket, or winner publication may follow separate event rules
public_features.note: For more detail about progression and visibility features, see

retention.title: 6. How Long We Keep Information
retention.intro: We retain information for different periods based on the type of data and the reason it is needed.

active.title: Active account data
active.intro: While an account remains active, we may keep:

long_term.title: Long-term operational records
long_term.intro: Some operational records may continue to be retained for:
long_term.note: In some cases, the platform may retain internal identifiers or pseudonymous records instead of names. Display-name snapshots in some historical records may be removed or anonymized after a retention period.

deleted.title: Deleted accounts
deleted.intro: If an account is deleted, direct identity data may be deleted or scrubbed, while some operational records may remain in anonymized or pseudonymous form for analytics, reporting, accounting, dispute handling, or legal compliance. Profile visibility controls are separate from deletion.
deleted.note: Internal historical progression handling on account deletion is still being finalized. Final implementation details may vary by subsystem and applicable law.

controls.title: 7. User Choices and Controls
controls.intro: Depending on the product surface, users may be able to control:
controls.note: These controls are not all the same. In particular:
notes: turning off personalized marketing is not the same as deleting the account
turning off leaderboard participation excludes the account from rankings
turning off public profile access is a separate control from leaderboard participation
turning off marketing does not turn off system alerts, and it does not automatically delete attendance or progression records
deleting the account may have broader consequences for profile and identity data

child_data.title: 8. Child and Dependent Data
child_data.intro: Some features support parent, dependent, or child-linked accounts. We treat child or dependent data with additional care. In particular:
items: child or dependent data is used for product operations, attendance, access, and related workflows
child or dependent data does not participate in progression, leaderboards, or public progression profiles
child or dependent data is not used for personalized marketing
parent or guardian relationships may be used to provide the service or communicate about dependent-linked activity

sharing.title: 9. Sharing and Disclosure
items: with service providers that help us operate the platform
with payment processors and infrastructure providers
with academy operators or authorized personnel as needed to provide academy services
when required by law, regulation, legal process, or valid governmental request
to protect the rights, safety, security, or integrity of the platform or others
sharing.note_2: The published policy may be updated as integrations and legal requirements change.

security.title: 10. Security
security.intro: We use reasonable administrative, technical, and organizational measures to protect information.
security.note: No method of transmission or storage is perfectly secure, and we cannot guarantee absolute security.

international.title: 11. International Use
international.intro: If the platform is used across different countries or regions, information may be processed in jurisdictions different from your own.
international.note: The final published policy should be reviewed for jurisdiction-specific requirements before release.

changes.title: 12. Changes To This Policy
changes.intro: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time.
changes.note: If we make material changes, we may update the effective date, post the revised policy, and take additional steps where appropriate.

contact.title: 13. Contact
contact.intro: For privacy-related questions or requests, contact:

deletion.title: Account Deletion
deletion.intro_primary: You can request deletion of your DojoMate account at any time, but the account still needs to pass the current validation checks before removal can finish.
deletion.intro_secondary: The Delete button is always available in the app. Pressing it runs the checks and shows anything that still needs to be resolved first.
deletion.in_app_text: In the DojoMate mobile app, go to Settings -> Privacy Settings -> Delete Account. Press Delete to run the current checks. The app will show unresolved items such as:
deletion.in_app_item_memberships: Active memberships or cards linked to you or to dependent child accounts.
deletion.in_app_item_obligations: Unpaid dues, pending booking fees, or other academy obligations that still need to be cleared.
deletion.in_app_item_access: Linked account conditions that still block removal, including dependent child accounts that cannot yet be deleted with the main account.
deletion.support_text: If you cannot satisfy the in-app checks yourself, for example because an academy still needs to close a card or clear a linked account condition, email support and ask for help reviewing the deletion blockers.
deletion.support_instructions: Include the email used for the account, your academy name, and whether you are a member or an academy owner. We will review the blockers and reply with the next steps after verification.
deletion.member_text: Member deletions are usually the simplest case once active cards, memberships, and linked-account blockers have been cleared. If your academy still owes unused value from a plan, deletion does not stop them from handling that separately through their own process.
deletion.owner_text: Owner accounts require extra review so we can protect academy members, settle wages, and preserve required history around subscriptions, payments, sessions, and fees.
deletion.owner_review: We manually review owner deletion requests before deactivating access. We will confirm what must be resolved first and which records remain retained for operations, compliance, or member-facing continuity.
deletion.data_text: Financial, attendance, and compliance-related records may still be retained after login removal where required for analytics, reporting, dispute handling, accounting, legal compliance, or operational continuity.

progression.title: Progression & Fair-Play Policy
progression.intro: DojoMate and Source Combatives use XP, levels, badges, streaks, and verified records to motivate consistent study for eligible self-managed accounts—not to sell in-app products. Progress is earned through recorded training and learning activity.
progression.fairness_text: Payments or subscriptions never grant XP, levels, badges, or rank eligibility. Payments provide access to learning content only; progress is earned through training actions and verified results.
progression.payments_text: DojoMate is a training-data terminal: it records class attendance, lesson views, and practice. DojoMate does not sell digital goods or subscriptions. Brick-and-mortar tuition may be paid through approved merchants (e.g., LINE Pay, Alipay) for in-person classes. Source Combatives handles online subscriptions and optional Extra Catalogs, which are paid on sourcecombatives.com only.
progression.xp_text_1: XP reflects eligible recorded activity in the live product. Current examples include:
progression.xp_list_watch: Completing eligible Education video content for the first time.
progression.xp_list_catalog: Completing eligible recorded Education units or curriculum segments.
progression.xp_list_attend: Attending in-person classes recorded in DojoMate.
progression.xp_list_tests: Passing in-person rank tests verified by instructors.
progression.xp_list_compete: Participating in recognized competitions (and podium results).
progression.xp_text_caps: Only activities explicitly marked as eligible in the live product count toward progression. Repeat viewing may grant limited practice XP, and caps may be used to reduce activity farming.
progression.extras_text: Some channels offer optional Extra Catalogs—focused learning modules. Purchasing an Extra Catalog grants access only. XP or badges appear only after completion. Extra Catalogs never alter rank requirements.
progression.homework_text: Homework uses auto-graded multiple-choice or true/false questions set by channels. There are no free-text submissions and no manual grading.
progression.ranks_text: All rank examinations are conducted in person. Online rank testing is not allowed. Eligibility depends on time, continuity, attendance, and instructor approval—not payment. Certificates are issued only after successful in-person evaluation.
progression.competitions_text: Verified competition participation and results from recognized organizations may appear according to event rules. Competition, bracket, and winner publication are separate from normal leaderboard participation and public profile controls. Entry fees alone do not grant XP.
progression.streaks_text: Streaks measure consistent weekly training, not daily logins. Grace periods and streak freezes exist for travel, exams, or injury.
progression.integrity_text: We use device/session limits, audit logs, and anomaly checks to protect fairness. Attempts to manipulate XP, ranks, or results may lead to removal of awards or account actions.
progression.privacy_text: Leaderboard participation, public profile access, and marketing personalization are separate controls. Opting out of leaderboard participation excludes the account from rankings. Public profile access controls broader public stats/profile visibility. Child or dependent records do not participate in progression, leaderboards, or public progression profiles. Internal historical progression handling on account deletion is still being finalized.
progression.appeals_prefix: If you believe a badge, XP entry, or rank record is incorrect, contact
progression.appeals_suffix: with details. We will review promptly.

help.title: Help Guide
help.text_1: Navigate using the table of contents on the left.
help.text_2: Type in keywoards to search for specific topics.

sign_in.title: Sign In
sign_in.intro: Use your email and password to sign in to DojoMate.
sign_in.action_sign_in: Tap Sign In to access your account.
sign_in.action_reset_password: Tap Reset Password if you forgot your password.
sign_in.tip_wrong_credentials: If login fails, double-check your email and password.
sign_in.tip_language_selector: Use the language selector at the top if needed.

create.title: Create Account
create.member: Member Account — for students and parents.
create.admin: Admin Account — for academy staff and owners.
create.email: A valid email address is required.
create.password_rules: Password must meet the app’s security rules.
create.next_member: Once created, you can join your academy as a member.
create.next_admin: Once created, you can manage academy staff, members, and schedules.

reset.title: Reset Password
reset.intro: If you forgot your password, use the Reset Password option on the sign-in screen.
reset.note_where: You’ll find Reset Password below the sign-in fields.
reset.note_email_timing: The reset email may take a few minutes to arrive.
reset.correct_email: Confirm you used the correct email.
reset.resend: Try again after a few minutes.

troubleshooting.title: Troubleshooting
troubleshooting.online: Ensure you are online; retry if the network was unstable.
troubleshooting.already_registered: If you previously signed up, use Sign In instead of Create Account.
troubleshooting.role_change: Signed up as Member but need Admin
troubleshooting.role_change_steps: Go to Settings → Account Settings. Scroll down to Admin Role, and select Admin.

change_password.title: Change Password
change_password.intro: Go to Settings → Account Settings, enter your old password and a new one, then press Change Password.
change_password.note: If the new password doesn’t work, use the Reset Password option instead.

language.title: Select Language
language.intro: Go to Settings → Account Settings. Tap the button that shows your current language, then choose your preferred language from the list.

currency.title: Select Currency
currency.intro: Go to Settings -> Account Settings, then press the button showing the currency symbol to choose your preferred currency.
currency.members: For members, subscription prices in the app will display in your chosen currency. Tuition payments always use your academy’s currency.
currency.owners: For academy owners, your selected currency becomes the default when creating a new academy, but it can be changed during setup.

logout.title: Log Out
logout.intro: Go to Settings → Account Settings and press the Log Out button.

privacy.title: Privacy Settings
privacy.intro: Go to Settings → Privacy Settings to control notifications, marketing, leaderboard participation, public profile access, and other visibility preferences.
privacy.notifications: System alerts include class cancellations and membership expirations. The app sends a push first; if delivery fails, an email is sent. You can turn off app notifications and/or emails.
privacy.marketing: Optional announcements from DojoMate or Source Combatives only—no third-party ads, no tracking, and we never sell your data. Marketing controls are separate from progression, leaderboard, and public-profile controls.
privacy.booking: When you book a class, your name appears on the attendance list. You may book anonymously to other students; admins and instructors still see your booking.
privacy.analytics_attendance: Attendance is recorded and not anonymous. This ensures accurate accountability, dispute resolution, and academy reporting.
privacy.analytics_aggregate: Other app metrics (e.g., membership card usage and trends) may be used in aggregate for product research and marketing insights. Child or dependent data is excluded from personalized marketing.

delete_account.title: Delete Account
delete_account.intro: Go to Settings → Privacy Settings, then press Delete under Delete Account.
delete_account.validations: Before deleting, ensure your academy has closed your account and canceled any active membership cards.
delete_account.failures: If deletion is blocked, one or more linked accounts may prevent removal.
delete_account.children: Dependent child accounts are deleted together with the main account when possible.
delete_account.child_cards: Child accounts with active membership cards must be cleared with their academy before the main account can be deleted.

edit_profile.title: Edit Profile
edit_profile.intro: Go to Profile → Edit Profile. Enter or change your profile information.
edit_profile.admin_role: Admin Role: If you created a Member account and want to become an Admin, select Admin here.

child_accounts.title: Child Accounts
child_accounts.parent_role: Member Role: Select Parent to unlock child account creation.
child_accounts.create: Press Create Child Account to add a child. The Create Account button appears after selecting a birthdate.
child_accounts.edit: Use the Child Accounts button to edit a child’s profile information.
child_accounts.delete: To delete: Press Delete Child Account to see the list of child accounts, then choose Delete Account under the child you wish to remove.

join.title: Join Academy
join.intro: Go to Academy → Search Academy to find and join your academy.
join.discover: You can also press Discover Academy to open a page that lists all academies in DojoMate.
join.qr: Allow camera usage and scan the academy’s QR code, provided by the admin.
join.search: You can also search by typing the academy name; partial matches will appear.
join.verify: Check details such as owner name and address before joining.
join.apply: Press Join Academy and wait for admin approval.
join.children: If you have dependent child accounts, admins can accept them afterwards.

memberships.title: Managing Memberships
memberships.intro: Go to Academy → Member Cards to see all active membership cards grouped by academy.
memberships.multi: You can join multiple academies; switch between them in Academy → Academy List.
memberships.select: If multiple academies are joined, select one as active for class booking.

booking.title: Booking Classes
booking.intro: First, ensure you’ve selected the correct academy in Academy → Academy List.
booking.schedule: Go to Academy → Schedule. Select a day to view lessons. Navigate weeks with the arrows.
booking.book: Press the Booking button next to a lesson. If it’s grayed out, the lesson is unpublished—contact your academy’s admin.
booking.confirm: On the attendance page, press Book Lesson. Then select a membership card.
booking.eligibility: If no cards appear, your card may not be eligible. Admins decide which card types (adult, child, beginner, etc.) can book each lesson.
booking.select_card: Tap a card until it turns blue, then press Confirm to book.
booking.unbook: Tap a booked card until it turns white, then press Confirm to unbook.

messages.title: System Messages
messages.intro: Go to Academy → System Messages to see notifications about your memberships, cards, and lessons.
messages.examples: Examples: card expired, new card issued, lesson cancelled. Parent accounts also see child account alerts.

records.title: Attendance Records
records.intro: Go to Academy → Records to view attendance history for yourself or your child accounts.
records.range: You can select a date range to filter records.
records.quick: Quick Access shows your most recently and previously used membership cards with linked lesson history.

create_academy.title: Create Academy
create_academy.intro: Go to Business → Create Academy. Enter academy information; more details help students find you.
create_academy.duplicates: Academy Name must be unique. If saving fails, a duplicate name may be the reason.
create_academy.currency: Currency defaults from your Settings. Choose your local currency now—this cannot be changed later and should match how students pay tuition.

academy_basics.title: Academy Basics
academy_basics.edit: Business -> Edit Academy lets you update academy details (e.g., address, contact, description).
academy_basics.messages: Business -> System Messages shows academy-level alerts similar to member messages

academy_qr.title: Academy QR Code
academy_qr.intro: Open the academy’s QR so students can scan and join.
academy_qr.multiple: If you manage multiple academies, press the button to open the QR Code page. Then choose Selected Academy to load the academy you want. Only one academy can be active at a time.

delete_academy.title: Delete Academy
delete_academy.preconditions: There must be no active membership cards. Clear cards with students first—academies cannot be deleted with active cards.
delete_academy.members: Reject all members before deletion. You cannot reject members who still have active cards.
delete_academy.success: After clearing cards and rejecting members, you can delete the academy.

add_instructor.title: Add Instructors
add_instructor.intro: Go to Business → Instructors. Press Choose Member, select a member or yourself, then press Add to Staff.
add_instructor.requirements: You must have at least one instructor to create lessons.
add_instructor.owner_tip: Most owners are the head instructor—add yourself first.
add_instructor.staff_list: Selected members appear in the staff list.
add_instructor.instructor_toggle: Turn the toggle on to make that staff member an instructor.
add_instructor.assistant_toggle: Leave the toggle off if this person should only be available as an assistant instructor.
add_instructor.analytics_active: Instructors is the page for managing who teaches. You may also see Analytics as a separate Business page.
add_instructor.next_steps: See the next section for instructions on adding members to your academy.

create_template.title: Create Card Template
create_template.intro: Go to Cards → Create Card Template and enter the details.
create_template.fields_name: Card Name — the template’s display name.
create_template.fields_fee: Fee — enter a number. The system will use the currency you chose when setting up the academy.
create_template.fields_sessions: Total Sessions — total lessons the card provides.
create_template.fields_expire_days: Expire Days — duration in days (e.g., 30 for a month, 365 for a year).
create_template.fields_type: Type — group or private lesson. Booking eligibility uses this type. Instructor hourly wages also depend on this.

edit_templates.title: Edit Card Templates
edit_templates.intro: Go to Cards → Edit Card Template to update details.
edit_templates.name_locked: Card Name cannot be changed to prevent mix-ups.
edit_templates.effects_note: Changes do not affect already issued cards (see Active Cards for adjustments).

delete_templates.title: Delete Card Templates
delete_templates.intro: Go to Cards → Delete Card Template, open the template, then press Delete Card.
delete_templates.cannot_delete_active: Templates with active cards cannot be deleted.

issue_cards.title: Issue Membership Cards
issue_cards.choose_template: First you will see Choose a Card to Assign. Press a card template to continue.
issue_cards.choose_member: Next you will see Choose Member.
issue_cards.newest_priority: Newly accepted members appear at the top for 24 hours to make them easier to find.
issue_cards.select_members: Select one or more members. Then scroll to the bottom and press Confirm.
issue_cards.final_confirm: Review the selected members, then press Confirm again to issue the cards.
issue_cards.already_issued_note: Members who already hold an active card for this template are shown in gray.
issue_cards.cannot_revoke_here: Issuance cannot be revoked on this screen.

manage_cards.title: Manage Membership Cards
manage_cards.intro_paths: Open via Cards → Active Cards (select a card) or via Members → Members List → Manage Cards.
manage_cards.actions: Available actions: Adjust Lessons, Adjust Expiry Time.
manage_cards.input_rules: Use positive numbers to increase and negative numbers to decrease lessons.
manage_cards.expiry_rule_note: Expiry can only be extended (days are added from 0); it cannot be reduced.

delete_cards.title: Delete Cards
delete_cards.intro_paths: Open via Cards → Active Cards → Select Action, or via Members → Members List → Manage Cards.
delete_cards.expired_default: Default reason: Card Expired — normal completion (lessons used or time expired).
delete_cards.no_duplicate_active: A member cannot hold two active cards for the same template. Delete the active card before issuing a new one.
delete_cards.cancel_option: Choose Cancel Card to remove a card that wasn’t fully used.
delete_cards.cancel_reason_required: Provide a note (e.g., issued wrong card, refund amount) — the reason is saved in records.
delete_cards.records_visibility: Expired cards do not appear in app-facing records; canceled cards do and include who canceled and the note.

card_records.title: Card Records
card_records.intro: Go to Cards → Card Records. First choose a record type, then use the search bar to filter by member.
card_records.empty_state: If no results appear, the member has no records for the selected type.
card_records.items_current: Current Card Records — attendance linked to the most recent active card.
card_records.items_issued: Issued Card Records — all active card issuances and who issued them.
card_records.items_canceled: Canceled Card Records — canceled cards, who canceled, and the recorded reason.
card_records.items_debt: Lesson Debt Records — entries created when lessons exceed remaining balance.
card_records.debt_policy: The system allows up to −3 lessons (maximum debt of 3). When a new card is issued, debt is settled by subtracting from the new card.
card_records.settlement_edge: If settling debt would leave the new card at 1 or fewer lessons, it is not auto-settled to avoid immediate re-renewal after one class.
card_records.manual_resolve: To resolve manually: open Lesson Debt Records, press Pay Off Debt for an entry, confirm the amount (optional note), then the entry shows Debt Cleared.
card_records.cleared_visibility: Cleared debt remains visible in records, without the Pay Off Debt action.

enrollment.title: Quick Enrollment
enrollment.intro: Owners can quickly register users without a DojoMate account, or add existing accounts using their email for DojoMate.
enrollment.portal_instructions: Go to the Affiliate Portal, log in using your DojoMate email and password, then press [Register].
enrollment.temp_password: Enter their real email address, and create a temporary password.
enrollment.roles_children: Choose the member role—parent or student. When selecting parent, another screen will appear to create child accounts.
enrollment.confirm: Review the information to ensure it is correct, then press [Confirm].
enrollment.view_members: You can now see the new members by pressing [Members].
enrollment.issue_cards: You can issue membership cards for the new members using the DojoMate app only.
enrollment.existing_account: If a new member already has a DojoMate account, you can add them by pressing [Member], then [Add Member by Email], entering their email, and pressing [Add].

prologue.title: Affiliate Portal
prologue.intro_1: Free owner tools are available in the
prologue.intro_2: Log in using your DojoMate email and password.
prologue.requirement: You must create at least one academy in DojoMate before you can use the Affiliate Portal.
prologue.available: The Affiliate Portal includes free owner tools such as Register, Members, Ranks, Cards, and Reports.

register.title: Register
register.intro: Use Register for quick onboarding. Accounts created here do not use the normal pending and accepted steps.
register.parent_flow: If you choose Parent, the next screen is for child account details. Enter the child information, then press Next.
register.student_flow: If you choose Student, press Next to go to the review page.
register.review: On the review page, check the information carefully, then press Confirm to create the account.
register.result: The account is created right away.
register.resume: If you leave after creating a parent account, you can resume child account creation later. The parent appears in Members under Pending Users. Press Resume to continue creating child accounts.

members.title: Members
members.intro: Use Members to manage member status in one place.
members.add_email: Press Add Member by Email if the account already exists in DojoMate.
members.accept_reject: You can accept or reject members from this page.
members.recently_accepted: Recently Accepted is a convenience list for recent changes. It can include newly accepted members and returning members after membership is paused or unpaused.
members.sections: There are six sections: Recently Accepted, Pending Users, Accepted Parent Accounts, Accepted Adult Members, Rejected Users, and Banned Users.
members.parents: Accepted Parent Accounts shows parents with linked child accounts.
members.adults: Accepted Adult Members shows accepted members without dependent child accounts.

ranks.title: Ranks
ranks.intro: Use Ranks to manage program rosters and save rank or stripe changes for each martial art.
ranks.programs: Add programs on the Ranks page, such as Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu or Judo. Each program keeps its own rank system and roster.
ranks.first_program: When you add the first program, all currently accepted academy members are added to that program automatically, but only as a one-time setup convenience and only if no enrollments already exist for that martial art.
ranks.later_members: Members who join later are not added automatically. You must add them to the program roster yourself.
ranks.additional_programs: When you add a second or later program, it starts empty. Existing members are not added automatically.
ranks.roster_view: The page shows one active program tab at a time. The roster only shows members enrolled in that program.
ranks.roster_actions: Use Add Members to enroll accepted academy members who are not already in the active program. You can also remove members from the active program roster.
ranks.save_direct: For members already in the roster, first-time rank assignment and forward progression can be saved directly.
ranks.forward_rule: Forward progression means moving to a higher rank in the same system, or increasing stripes on the same rank.
ranks.review_needed: Rank demotions, stripe decreases, switches to a different rank system in that row, and resets or corrections require review before saving.
ranks.review_modal: In the review window, choose a change type and enter notes. Allowed change types are correction, demotion, and reset. Confirm stays unavailable until both are entered.
ranks.guardrails: If a member is not enrolled in the selected program roster, rank changes cannot be saved.
ranks.history: Rank history is recorded whenever changes are saved.
ranks.bootstrap_note: The first-program auto-enrollment is only for initial setup. After enrollment rows exist, the page works in explicit roster mode.

cards.title: Cards
cards.intro: Use Cards to manage templates, card assignment, active cards, and records.
cards.tabs: There are four tabs: Cards, Assign, Active, and History.
cards.cards_tab: Cards is where you create, edit, and delete card templates.
cards.assign_tab: Assign is where you issue active cards to members using your card templates.
cards.active_tab: Active is where you manage active cards, including actions such as deleting or pausing cards.
cards.history_tab: History shows card records in one page.
cards.history_search: Use the Search Records field at the top to filter records.
cards.history_lists: History lists issued card records, deleted card records, and audit logs together.

reports.title: Reports
reports.intro: Use Reports to view monthly financial reports.
reports.items: Reports show basic financial information such as cost, revenue, and profit.
reports.active_only: Reports are generated only while the tool is active.
reports.inactive_missing: If the tool is inactive, such as unpaid or not on a free trial, reports for that time will not be created and will be missing here.
reports.future_note: A future update will allow past financial data to be viewed even when the tool is inactive. Try to keep your subscription active if you want a complete report history.

applications.title: Academy Applications
applications.intro: Users can also apply to join your academy from the Join Academy page.
applications.instructions: Go to Members → Add/Reject Members to view pending applications.
applications.no_pending: If you see “No pending accounts”, the member didn’t apply correctly. Ask them to try again by scanning your academy QR code or searching your academy name.
applications.accept_reject: When an application appears, press Accept or Reject.
applications.child_accounts: To accept linked child accounts, go to Members → Members List, open the parent’s profile, and accept child accounts listed there.
applications.refresh: If child accounts don’t appear, either they weren’t created (or creation failed) or your list is stale. Ask the parent to create them, or close and relaunch the app to refresh (no need to log out).

rejection.title: Rejecting or Banning Members
rejection.intro: Manage membership status from the member’s profile.
rejection.reject: Go to Members → Members List, open a profile, and press Reject to remove a member. You cannot reject members with active cards—clear their account and delete active cards first.
rejection.rejected_list: Find rejected members in Members → Rejected List. From there you can Accept or Ban.
rejection.ban: Banned members cannot reapply by pressing Join Academy.
rejection.unban: To remove a ban, go to Members → Banned List, press their name, and press Remove Ban. They will reappear in Add/Reject Members for acceptance.

records.title: Attendance Records
records.intro: View attendance records for members and linked child accounts.
records.date_range: Select a date range. If only the first date is filled, results show that single day. Adding a second date retrieves the whole range.
records.clear_field: Once the second date is touched, it defaults to today. You can choose a date before today. To clear it, reload the page.
records.member_search: After setting the range, press Choose Member.
records.view_records: A window opens and lists members with attendance records for that time period. Press a name to view that member’s attendance records. Screenshot for recordkeeping or dispute resolution.

create.title: Create Lesson
create.intro: Go to Schedule. It opens on today by default—select the day you want and press Create Lesson.
create.lesson_type: Lesson Type: defaults to Group Lesson.
create.lesson_type_note: Booked cards must match the lesson type (Group or Private).
create.date: Date: double-check if you navigated around the calendar.
create.time: Start / End Time: use 24-hour format (HH:MM). Times use your local timezone.
create.classroom: Classroom (optional): required if you schedule overlapping lessons.
create.max_capacity: Maximum Capacity: required and must be > 0. Students cannot book beyond the limit; admins can.
create.min_capacity: Minimum Capacity: required and must be > 0. The lesson auto-cancels if not reached by the booking cut-off time.
create.cutoff: Booking Cut-Off: defaults to 5 minutes if empty. Suggested ≥ 20 minutes so students get timely cancellation notices. After cut-off, students can’t book/unbook; admins can until the class finishes.
create.sessions_deduct: Sessions to Deduct: defaults to 1. You can set any lesson length and deduct a non-zero number if you need to charge more than 1 per lesson.
create.usable_cards: Usable Membership Cards: tap to select one or more cards allowed to book this lesson (e.g., adults, children, beginner/advanced).
create.instructors: Instructor: tap to select one or more; at least one is required. (See Add Instructors.)

manage.title: Edit / Cancel / Delete Lesson
manage.intro: Go to Schedule, select the date, then tap the lesson (not the Booking button).
manage.edit: Edit the lesson info and press Save Changes.
manage.cancel: Cancel Lesson: unbooks all currently booked members and prevents new booking. The Booking button will be disabled.
manage.delete: Delete Lesson: if you need to remove it entirely (e.g., created by mistake), cancel it first, then press Delete Lesson.
manage.cutoff_rule: You can’t edit a lesson after the booking cut-off time.
manage.started_rule: You can’t cancel or delete a lesson that has already started.

book.title: Booking for Students
book.intro: Encourage students/parents to book themselves. If cut-off has passed or they forgot, admins can book with more freedom (not limited by cut-off or capacity).
book.open: Go to Schedule → select date → tap the lesson → Booking → Add Members.
book.select_member: Find the member (parent or child). Tap to show their active cards that are allowed for this lesson.
book.choose_card: Tap a card until it turns blue, then press Confirm to book.
book.unbook: To unbook, tap the member in the attendance list, tap the blue (booked) card until it turns gray, then press Confirm.
book.finished_rule: You can’t book (or unbook) students into lessons that have finished.

prologue.title: Paid Tools
prologue.intro_1: Paid tools are purchased by academy owners at
prologue.intro_2: Other roles may use them for free. You can log in if you have created at least one academy.
prologue.available: There are four paid tools: Timetable, Pause Cards, Staff Access, and Analytics. The Premium Package includes all four at a discount, with a free 90-day trial available. Press Start Trial on the Features page to begin.
prologue.pricing: Prices are regionally adjusted (PPP). Enter your Country (and Province for China) to receive the correct discount.

timetable.title: Timetable
timetable.intro: Manage a weekly recurring schedule. Create lesson templates once and publish them directly to the calendar without re-creating each lesson.
timetable.create: Go to Timetable and select a day of the week. Press Create Lesson to open the same form as the Schedule screen and fill it out as you would for a normal lesson.
timetable.end_date: Timetable templates include an additional End Date field. Define a date range using Start Date (default today) and End Date (in the future). Templates do not exist until published, so you can safely set a far-future end date.
timetable.edit: After creation, lesson templates appear on the day-of-week page. Press a template to edit it, following the same process as regular lessons.
timetable.publish: Unpublished lessons appear on the Schedule page. Admins will see a Publish button instead of Booking—press to publish and allow booking. Members see unpublished lessons grayed out, which is useful for viewing future plans.
timetable.holidays: If a scheduled lesson falls on a holiday, you may simply choose not to publish it. Once published, lessons cannot be unpublished. To remove a published lesson, follow the same Cancel Lesson procedure as in the Schedule instructions.

pause_cards.title: Pause Cards
pause_cards.intro: You can pause a card to stop the clock when needed.
pause_cards.individual: Members → Accepted Members. Find or search the member, open the profile, then use the middle (orange) Pause Cards button to pause/unpause.
pause_cards.academy_wide: Owners can pause all cards for the academy: Cards → Pause Cards.
pause_cards.template_toggle: Each card template has a toggle to exclude it from academy-wide pauses (e.g., let Gift Cards continue expiring).
pause_cards.no_booking: Paused cards cannot be used to book lessons.
pause_cards.independence: Academy-wide pause/unpause does not change individual pause states; they are independent.

staff_permissions.title: Staff Access
staff_permissions.intro: Delegate tasks in the app to administrative staff by assigning access.
staff_permissions.add_staff: Go to Business → Permissions to grant or update admin access.
staff_permissions.set_permissions: There are two badges: Member and Admin. Member means the person does not currently have admin access.
staff_permissions.make_admin: Press a member’s name, then press Confirm to promote them to admin.
staff_permissions.schedule: Schedule Access — allows creating, editing, and booking lessons on the Schedule page.
staff_permissions.members: Members Access — allows Add/Reject/Ban/Unban. Checking attendance records (Members → Records) is always available to Admins and not restricted.
staff_permissions.cards: Cards Access — allows creating, editing, and deleting card templates; issuing, adjusting, or canceling active cards. Tuition is collected on card issuance. See Membership Cards → Card Records for audit logs.
staff_permissions.timetable: Timetable Access — allows creating, editing, deleting, and publishing lesson templates.
staff_permissions.confirm: After that, press the admin’s name to change access values as before. Choose Yes or No for each item and press Confirm.
staff_permissions.trial_expiry: If this tool is unavailable, you will not be able to change admin access here.
staff_permissions.remove_staff: Press Remove Admin to change an admin back into Member.

overview.title: Analytics Overview
overview.intro: Analytics is a separate Business page for metrics, compensation, and time or payroll tools.
overview.realtime: Real-time data includes: Active Members (with active cards), New/Lost Members this month, Total Planned Classes this week, Completed Classes this week, and Most Popular Card (by usage count).
overview.weekly_reports: Weekly reports summarize attendance and class performance, published every Monday for the previous week.
overview.weekly_items: Reports include total classes planned and completed, top attended classes, and most active members (one adult and one child).
overview.financial_reports: Weekly financial reports are released every Wednesday and include Weekly Net Profit and Average Income per Class.
overview.monthly_reports: Monthly reports include 'No Attendance Report', showing members with active cards who did not attend any class during the previous month.
overview.notes: Weekly reports only include teaching and staff costs (no rent, utilities, or sales). Financial reports are lesson-related only.

wages.title: Instructor & Staff Wages
wages.intro: Set salary or hourly rates for instructors and staff so the system can calculate costs accurately.
wages.access: Go to Business → Analytics to manage compensation details. Use Instructors to manage who teaches. Updating compensation in Analytics does not make someone an instructor.
wages.roles: If Is Instructor is selected in the salary form, the system counts teaching hours using that wage. If it is not selected, teaching hours are not counted for that staff member.
wages.assistant_note: Assistant instructors can still be selected for lessons even when Is Instructor is off.
wages.contract_types: Choose Contract Type: monthly, hourly, or both (for instructors only). You can set both a base monthly and hourly teaching rate.
wages.fields: Enter Monthly Rate, Hourly Rate, and Private Lesson Rate. Use your local currency set when the academy was created.
wages.private_rate: Private Lesson Rate applies only when the lesson type is Private. Group lessons use the standard rate.
wages.tiered_intro: Tiered Wages are optional and allow hourly pay to increase with class attendance size.
wages.tiered_example: Add tiers using + Add Tier or × Remove Tier. Example: Tier 1: 2 students → $20/hr, Tier 2: 5 students → $30/hr, Tier 3: 8 students → $40/hr.
wages.tiered_behavior: Attendance determines tier activation: 2–4 students use Tier 1, 5–7 use Tier 2, 8 or more always use Tier 3.
wages.minute_calculation: Lessons are not limited to 60 minutes. The system calculates hourly wages by minutes (e.g., 90-minute lesson = 1.5× hourly rate).

punch_cards.title: Staff Punch Cards
punch_cards.intro: Clock-in/out system for tracking staff office hours. Instructor teaching hours are calculated automatically from scheduled lessons.
punch_cards.usage: Go to Business → Analytics. Press Actions next to a staff member, then Clock In/Out.
punch_cards.rules: Staff on fixed salary can still clock in/out for attendance tracking. Hourly staff must clock in/out for pay calculation.
punch_cards.instructors_note: Instructors do not need to clock in unless you wish to monitor office hours. Their pay is based on lesson teaching time.
punch_cards.current_limit: Currently, only the academy owner can clock staff in/out. A future update will enable staff self check-in/out using an RFID card at the academy.
punch_cards.audit_intro: Timesheet Audit runs every Monday. The owner reviews and approves each punch card for the previous week.
punch_cards.audit_steps: Scroll to the bottom of the Analytics page and press Review next to each punch card to inspect details.
punch_cards.inspect: Check dates, hours, and clock-in/out times. Missing or incorrect data can be adjusted manually.
punch_cards.problems: If missing wage data, enter it. If staff forgot to clock out, press Clock Out. If hours are too long (forgot to clock out earlier), edit the working hours manually.
punch_cards.actions: Write notes for special cases (e.g., left early). To reject false or duplicate records, press Error → Reject. Press Mark as Late to log tardiness (tracking only).
punch_cards.approval: When correct, press Approve. Approved punch cards are included in financial reports.
punch_cards.summary: Every Monday: previous week’s punch cards appear for approval. Every 1st of the month: remaining partial week cards appear. Reports are generated on Wednesdays and the 3rd of each month, giving 2 days to complete reviews. Unapproved cards are excluded from reports.
